name: X4 Pharmaceuticals, Inc
business: X4 Pharmaceuticals is a biopharmaceutical company developing and commercializing novel therapeutics for the treatment of rare hematology diseases. It has one FDA-approved product, XOLREMDI (mavorixafor), for WHIM syndrome in patients 12 years and older. The company is also conducting a Phase 3 trial (4WARD) of mavorixafor for chronic neutropenia.

exposures.sentence: We are a biopharmaceutical company developing and commercializing novel therapeutics for the treatment of rare hematology diseases.

catalysts.sentence: We are targeting completion of enrollment in 4WARD in the third quarter of 2026...
expect to provide top-line data from our 4WARD trial in the second half of 2027.

catalysts.event: EC final approval decision for mavorixafor in WHIM syndrome in EU
catalysts.sentence: The positive opinion will now be reviewed by the EC with a final approval decision anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: FDA approval for mavorixafor in CN
catalysts.sentence: We expect to complete enrollment in the third quarter of 2026 and report top-line data results in the second half of 2027, with the goal of receiving FDA approval in 2028.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 57,000 patients with primary CN in the US.
tamClaims.sentence: Our market research with 100 hematologists and oncologists treating patients with primary CN indicates that of the approximately 57,000 patients with primary CN (identified via ICD-10 codes associated with primary CN in database of medical claims from the 1/2023 - 6/2025 time frame,)

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 34,000 patients have moderate or severe CN.
tamClaims.sentence: an estimated 60%, or approximately 34,000, of these patients have moderate or severe disease, which is defined as patients having absolute neutrophil counts of 1000 cells/ L.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 15,000 CN patients experience serious and/or recurring infections.
tamClaims.sentence: Of these moderate and severe patients, hematologists and oncologists surveyed estimated that approximately 45%, or approximately a further subset of 15,000 patients, are experiencing serious and/or recurring infections as result of their CN.

tamClaims.claim: Initial target treatment population is approximately 5,000 patients.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that approximately 5,000 of the 15,000 patients will represent our initial target treatment population.
